subtasks.description: Inside the existing <style> block, add rules for .privacy-badge (light-green tint background, border, flex layout, small font), .privacy-pills (flex-wrap row for the three pills), .pill (dot separator via ::before pseudo-element on .pill + .pill), and .view-source (underlined, bold, matching green color). Include a @media (max-width: 480px) block that switches .privacy-badge to column layout and tightens pill gap for narrow viewports.

status_history.message: Build orchestration started and worktree prepared.
Planning completed and implementation subtasks were generated.

Started subtask 1/2: Add styles for a .privacy-badge component inside the existing <style> block. Style it as a small, non-intrusive banner below the tagline: subtle background (e.g. light green tint), small font, inline icon characters (no external icon fonts), flex layout for the three claim pills (no cookies · no tracking · no account), and a 'View Source' anchor. Include a @media (max-width: 480px) rule to stack or wrap the pills on narrow viewports.

data.subtaskDescription: Add styles for a .privacy-badge component inside the existing <style> block. Style it as a small, non-intrusive banner below the tagline: subtle background (e.g. light green tint), small font, inline icon characters (no external icon fonts), flex layout for the three claim pills (no cookies · no tracking · no account), and a 'View Source' anchor. Include a @media (max-width: 480px) rule to stack or wrap the pills on narrow viewports.

status_history.message: Started subtask 2/2: Insert a <div class="privacy-badge"> element in the .container, immediately after the .tagline paragraph. The badge must contain three visible claim tokens — '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required' — and a 'View Source' link using the view-source: protocol (view-source: + location.href rendered via a small inline script attribute or a static relative path) so users can inspect the full HTML in-browser and verify the zero-external-requests guarantee themselves.

data.subtaskDescription: Insert a <div class="privacy-badge"> element in the .container, immediately after the .tagline paragraph. The badge must contain three visible claim tokens — '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required' — and a 'View Source' link using the view-source: protocol (view-source: + location.href rendered via a small inline script attribute or a static relative path) so users can inspect the full HTML in-browser and verify the zero-external-requests guarantee themselves.

status_history.message: AI review completed. Awaiting human review and PR decision.

validations: Privacy badge is rendered directly in the HTML with no user interaction required
Three claim pills present: '🔒 No cookies', '🚫 No tracking', '👤 No account required'
View Source link uses view-source: protocol set via inline script (document.getElementById('view-source-link').href = 'view-source:' + location.href)
No external scripts, CDN fonts, analytics, or tracking resources were introduced
Responsive CSS: flex-wrap on pills and a @media (max-width: 480px) block stacks the badge to column layout on narrow viewports
Badge uses subtle light-green tint (#f0faf2 background, #c3e6cb border) — non-intrusive styling consistent with the spec
Badge is positioned immediately after the .tagline paragraph as specified in the subtasks
